sutta: at 1 time the exalted 1 dwelt near vesali at the gabled hall in maharavana and only 1 morning after dressing the exalted 1 took bowl and roped and entered vey sally for arms and having gone his round on his return after the midday meal he made his way into the great forest of mahavana and sat down for the noon day rest at the foot of a tree now at that time a company of lichavi young men were out stalking and ranging in mahavana they had their bows strung and were surrounded by a pack of dogs and they saw the exalted 1 seated at the foot of the tree then at that site they cast aside their bows called off their dogs and approached the exalted 1 saluted him and stood with hands upraised in silence reverencing him just then mahanama the lichavi was stretching his legs in mahavana by walking up and down and he saw those young lithavis with upraised hands silently reverencing the exalted 1 and mahanama the litavi went to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said with baited breath they will become vaginas they will become bajans and the buddha said but why mahanama do you speak so they will become vaginas lord these lithavi young men are quick tempered rough greedy fellows such presence as are sent by clan folk jujubee fruit sweet cakes sweet meats and lollipops they go about plundering and eating they slap the women and girls of the clan on the back such are these fellows who now with upraised hands stand in silence reverencing the exalted 1 and the buddha said wheresoever these 5 conditions are found mahanama whether in a crown warrior raja or in a countryman living on his paternal farm or in a general of an army or in a governor of villages or in a guild master or in those who make themselves the sole power among the clans growth may be expected and not declined what 5 take the case mahanama of a clan's men who with wealth gotten by work and zeal gathered by the strength of the arm earned by the sweat of the brow justly obtained in a lawful way he honors reveres venerates and references his parents at once his parents honored revered venerated and reverenced fondly regard him with loving thoughts and say long life to you and may your long life be protected to the clansmen mahanama who has formed regard of his parents growth may be expected and not declined so too mahanama to the clansmen who has formed regard of his children and wife his slaves work men and folk and work workforce and men growth may be expected and not declined to the clansmen who has fallen regard of the labourers in these fields and those whose business is with the boundaries growth may be expected and not declined to the clansmen mahanama who has foreign regard of the devas celestial beings who are accustomed to receive offerings growth may be expected and not declined moreover mahanama a clansman who with wealth gotten by work and zeal gathered by the strength of the arm earned by the sweat of the brow justly obtained in a lawful way honors reviews venerates and reverences recluses and brahmins will at once by them so honored be regarded with compassion with benevolence and they will say long life to you and may your long life be protected to the clansmen mahanama who is regarded with compassion by recluse and brahmins growth may be expected and not declined wherever these 5 conditions are found mahanama whether in a crown warrior raja a countryman living on his paternal farm a general of an army a governor of villages a guild master or indoors who make themselves the sole power among the clans growth may be expected and not declined that's the end of the sutta
commentary: here in this utah the buddha went to mahavana vana means like a forest maha is great so it's a great foresla and there there was these young little young young little men who went hunting and when they saw the buddha sitting underneath the tree they they instinctly felt that this was a holy man so they put down their bows and called out their dogs and went to pay respect to the buddha and this mahanama the lichavi probably 1 of the followers of the buddha he said he saw this was being very unusual because he said these these little young men and generally very rough greedy fellows quick tempered and all that but he was very surprised that they are paying respect to the buddha so the buddha said whoever no matter what kind of person if he has these 5 factors then he may progress the growth may be expected first 1 he has foreign regard of his parents and secondly has foreign regard of his family children and wife his slaves and workers thirdly the laborers in his fields and those who do business with him fourthly the deva celestial beings that means making offerings to them and fifthly renuncian he has formed regard for monks also then the buddha said now with these 5 conditions whoever he may be he is bound to make progress because when he has foreign regard for these people they all wish him well and and if a person is well regarded by others he has he has a lot of meta and a meter a lot of loving kindness goes back to him then he should progress now that means a person a person basically a good person must have love in his heart if a person has love in his heart for other people then he is a good person and it doesn't matter whether he appears a very rough person a greedy person etc the most important human qualities we must have if we have if we have these basic human qualities basic good human qualities like love in our heart then um we we can be confident that we are going the right way it's not only wisdom that's important some people think meditation is all important these basic human qualities are very important on the nexus [Music]
